summary: coder v6's F-A fix (persistent-grant durability) is correct and the suite is green — C# 2854/2854, PLang 203/203 after the app-lowercase merge. But the SkipFreshnessCheck flag neutralises TWO independent checks (step 2 wire-freshness, step 4 nonce-replay) and only the step-2 half has a test. The new Scenario4 durability test advances NowUtc +10 min so it dies on step 2's age check; it never re-presents a nonce. Mutation true->false kills exactly 1 test. F1 (major): no test verifies a persisted grant twice — a regression re-enabling step 4 for grants would survive the full suite and silently re-break 'always allow' for any app that re-reads a foreign resource. Confirmed by a scratch probe (app2 reads twice: re-prompts under mutation, covered with the fix). One added test closes it; result.md hands over the exact test, mutation-verified.

findings.title: Nonce-replay half of the F-A fix is ungated
findings.detail: SkipFreshnessCheck=true skips Ed25519 step 2 (wire-freshness) AND step 4 (nonce-replay). Only step 2 is tested. Persisted Find re-deserializes a fresh Data each call (SettingsStore.GetAll), so two Finds = two VerifySignature passes; with step 4 active the second hits NonceReplay and re-prompts. No test does two verifications of a persisted grant. A step-4-only revert passes all 2854 tests.
findings.fix: Add Scenario4_PersistedGrantReVerified_NonceReplayDoesNotReprompt (full test in v5/result.md) — app1 creates a persisted grant, app2 reads twice with a StatelessChannel, assert neither read is Type 'ask'. Mutation-verified by tester: fails under SkipFreshnessCheck=false, passes with the fix.

non_blocking_notes.detail: ValidatePathTests.UpperCasedRootPrefix_..._OnUnix docstring still over-claims the RootComparison gate (carried from tester v4 N1).

auditor F-5 (attributed to tester): MoveCopyBundledConsentTests covers bundled consent only on the v2 Path surface; the real copy.cs/move.cs handlers issue two prompts. Add a note or a handler-path test. Fair to defer with F-C/D/E.
